statement.informal: Division of two positive reals is computable in 2 F16 nodes via the chain D_F16(x, D_F13(−1, y)). The inner D_F13(−1, y) computes y⁻¹; the outer D_F16 computes x · y⁻¹ = x/y.
statement.formal_lean:
theorem div_two_node_pos_domain (x y : ℝ) (hx : 0 < x) (hy : 0 < y) :
    D_F16 x (D_F13 (-1) y) = x / y

tactics: have hinv : D_F13 (-1 : ℝ) y = y⁻¹ := by ...
rw [hinv]; unfold D_F16; rw [show Real.log y⁻¹ = -(Real.log y) from Real.log_inv y]
rw [Real.exp_add, Real.exp_log hx, Real.exp_neg, Real.exp_log hy]; field_simp

common_mistakes.why_fails: Trying to chain everything into one `simp` — works occasionally but obscures which identity is doing what.
Using `ring` instead of `field_simp` — would fail because ring does not handle the (−log y) inversion automatically.
